provenance.what: Client-equivalent Maia-3 policy `maia_prob` (probability of played_uci after legal-move-masked softmax) per (fen, pinned_elo).
provenance.reference_path: onnxruntime-web WASM execution provider (the exact runtime the browser Maia worker ships) + the LIVE frontend encoding frontend/src/lib/maiaEncoding.ts (encodeBoard + maskAndSoftmax).
provenance.elo_convention: elo_self == elo_oppo == pinned_elo (symmetric-strength sweep, matching maia-worker.js analyze()).
provenance.maia_prob_definition: POLICY probability of played_uci (post maskAndSoftmax over logits_move), NOT the WDL expected score. This is the value classifyGem's C1 check (gemMove.ts GEM_MAIA_MAX_PROB=0.20) consumes.
provenance.tier_edges: gem: maia_prob <= 0.20; great: 0.20 < maia_prob <= 0.50; neither: maia_prob > 0.50 (from frontend/src/lib/gemMove.ts + the Great ceiling in CONTEXT.md).
provenance.non_circularity: These expected values come from the TS encoding + onnxruntime-web WASM path, which is INDEPENDENT of the Python port under test (app/services/maia_encoding.py + onnxruntime CPU). The parity gate therefore measures genuine encoding-port fidelity + CPU-vs-WASM float drift, not a self-comparison.
provenance.anchor_cross_check: The 2600-ELO Qxh2+/Rxd1 pair reproduces the independently UI-captured values in .planning/notes/2026-07-10-flawchess-engine-self-execution-analysis.md (UI: Qxh2+ 0.4524, Rxd1 0.4184; here: 0.4494, 0.4222) within WebGPU-vs-WASM drift.
provenance.coverage: 4 great, 4 gem, 3 neither; minimum margin from a tier edge is 0.033 (Nf3 @1500).
provenance.measured_max_drift: 0.003844 (2026-07-16, Python onnxruntime CPU vs onnxruntime-web WASM reference, 11-entry corpus; max on Rxd1 @2600).
provenance.parity_epsilon: 0.010 (scripts/maia_parity_spike.py PARITY_EPSILON) — ~2.6x headroom over measured max drift, well below the 0.033 tightest tier-edge margin so no tier flip can hide in the band.
